title: These Home Hacks Will Change Your Life

chunks.title: Introduction and Overview
chunks.summary: The speaker welcomes viewers to their house in Costa Rica and sets the stage for a tour focused on health hacks. They emphasize that the tips shared may seem unconventional but are aimed at improving health and well-being.
chunks.transcript: welcome to my house in Costa Rica yes I invited you and you are here with me I'm going to give you a little mini walkthrough tour and show you all of the health hacks in my house there will definitely be some Surprises by the end of the video you might think I'm even crazier than you do right now but watch till the end to make sure you get all of these Health acts because I hope that each and every one of them will improve your life come on guys let's

chunks.title: Kitchen Health Hacks
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their practices in the kitchen, highlighting the use of wood cutting boards, glass containers, and a stainless steel juicer. They discuss the importance of avoiding plastic and the benefits of consuming fresh fruit juices.
chunks.transcript: welcome to my kitchen we will start here and I will show you some of my home health hacks start with my cutting boards there are zero plastic cutting boards in my house that's a lie chunks.title: Meat Preparation and Quality
chunks.summary: The speaker details how they make their own ground beef using a hand grinder and discuss sourcing meats, including grass-fed options and organ meats. They also mention their company's animal-based protein products.
chunks.transcript: I make my own ground beef so I will get cuts of steak from the butcher that are affordable chuck roast or a chuck roll I'll cut them up myself into small pieces and I can mix as much fat or as little fat as I want make my own ground beef this is entirely um stainless steel this thing weighs probably 15 12 or 14 PBS I can also grind organs into my meat if I want with the meat grinder if you don't want to grind organs into your meat that's why what we do what we do at lineage we've got our meat sticks with organs in there grass-fed grass finished air dried meat sticks and our new animal based complete protein powder I'll show you my

chunks.title: Water Filtration and Hydration
chunks.summary: The speaker explains their water filtration system using reverse osmosis, emphasizing its purity and the addition of minerals using microplastic-free sea salt. They address common concerns regarding reverse osmosis water.
chunks.transcript: countertop reverse osmosis water piter most of what I drink is fruit juice watermelon juice mango juice raw cfir but if I'm going to use water I'll do reverse osmosis and I use a microplastic free sea salt to remineralize it people get kind of bent out of shape about reverse osmosis it's dead water yeah it's it's just H2O because you removed almost everything from that water which means you removed pesticides you removed Pharmaceuticals you removed organic pollutants you removed heavy metals you removed bacteria and viruses and a ton of stuff that you don't want so it's very very pure water and I'm going to add back in some minerals with the sea salt and I think that's totally totally fine since we're getting totally

chunks.title: Cleaning and Household Products
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses their preferences for natural cleaning products, emphasizing the use of baking soda instead of traditional soaps. They explain how they clean fruits and maintain hygiene without commercial products.
chunks.transcript: cotton dish towels because I hate paper towels I hate wasting paper towels and I'm not going to use a polyester dish towel I use baking soda to clean my dishes and I use a natural fiber brush so this is a bamboo handle and the bristles believe it or not are made from bores hair basically I just think about microplastics and home contamination as much as possible and I try to minimize that in my home so I don't use mainstream dish soap baking soda is the best hack ever it's super cheap I use it to clean my produce so you guys know I don't eat vegetables but I do eat fruit and you can use I think it's a teaspoon of baking soda per liter of water for 15 minutes if you soak your fruit it neutralizes a significant amount I think the study shows between 70 and 99% of the pesticides on the surface of fruit so baking soda is the best way to detoxify to clean your fruit I always have baking soda it's not creatine I have baking soda next to my sink because that's how I wash my dishes not with dish soap and that's how I wash my fruit

chunks.title: Personal Care and Hygiene Practices
chunks.summary: In this segment, the speaker shares their personal care routine, avoiding chemical-laden products such as traditional toothpaste and using natural alternatives like baking soda. They express concerns about microplastics and their potential health impacts.

chunks.title: Technology and EMF Exposure
chunks.summary: The speaker describes their approach to technology in the home, specifically avoiding Wi-Fi to minimize EMF exposure. They highlight the use of ethernet connections and discuss their nighttime routine with red light for better health.
chunks.transcript: so this is my Wi-Fi router and as you can see it's just a mess I probably should build a case here to to to hide it all but there's actually no Wi-Fi in my house this is really an internet ethernet router so all of these are ethernet cords and then I have a splitter here for extras so what I've done is I've just taken cat 6 cable and put on adapters so I can run my cell phone and my computer on ethernet instead of using Wi-Fi in my house why I'm still not convinced that RF EMF so radio frequency EMF which is a lot like what comes out of your microwave is safe for humans and one of the reasons I love lion Costa Rica is that there's no cell service at my house and I can completely do this I can completely shut down Wi-Fi at my house so there's essentially very very little to know radio frequency EMF in my house you can plug this one in your computer uh that's a USBC to use the internet and you can plug this one into your iPhone I still have an iPhone 13 because I'm a trog and uh that's a lightning connector but you can if if you had an iPhone 15 you could use the same connector for your USBC iPhone and your computer and you would only need one maybe I'll upgrade at some point but right now I have a lightning to ethernet connector a lot of people don't even know that exists and if you guys are wondering yes this one is covered in black electrical tape because it lights up blue when you use it and so at night in my house as I've shown in the past I'm like my nighttime routine videos I just use red lights they're incandescent red lights or non-flickering LEDs and I don't want this this flickering blue light on this thing is kind of a pain when I'm using my phone at night so just trying to create the most chill atmosphere at night in my house and that's why that's that way so

chunks.title: Grilling Techniques and Health
chunks.summary: The speaker showcases their stainless steel grill and explains the cooking techniques that avoid harmful compounds. They discuss how cooking methods can affect food quality and suggest using herbs like rosemary for added benefits.
chunks.transcript: stainless steel grill I actually brought this thing to Costa Rica and what is cool about it the food cooking platform is completely stainless steel so the food gets cooked only on stainless steel and I'll let you guys take a look inside here it the flame is on top of the food and there's a ceramic element that heats up so there are no flames below the food one of the things that people are concerned about with regular grills when the Flames are below the food the fat from the food drips down onto the Flames the Flames come up and they hit the food and that creates more polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons or heterocyclic amines so I think it's nice to have your flame above the food with a ceramic element like this because there's no drip there's no flare of the Flames onto your food and I try not to burn my food if I'm really worried about polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons or heterocyclic amines I can add a little bit of rosemary to my meat it's a spice it's a plant yes I know but a little bit doesn't seem to bother me and that has been shown in studies to decrease the formation of polycyclic aromatic hydar heterocylic amans if you're very worried about the compounds that are produced when you grill Meats don't look at what's in your coffee because coffee also contains polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons and heterocyclic amines I'll save that for a different video all

chunks.title: Fitness and Outdoor Lifestyle
chunks.summary: The final segment illustrates the speaker's fitness regimen, including outdoor exercises and specific equipment like a backwards treadmill. They emphasize the benefits of staying active and connected to nature in Costa Rica.
chunks.transcript: the gym I'll show you a couple of my favorite things in the gym right off the bat uh that bar gets used more than anything else so I hang I do pull-ups and I work on hip flexors so I'll show you how I do this so I like to hang throughout the day just to open the spine and the shoulders can do pull-ups and my favorite way to train ABS right now is that it's harder than it looks so give it a try got my Nordic bench here but I'll show you guys my atg backwards treadmill this thing is great for sessions between work sessions where I'm just sitting around doing stuff or not moving shout out to Ben Patrick I think it's interesting the way this works the ligaments in your knees differently and yeah backwards treadmill genius throw in a Nordic for good measure obviously I like to be outside too get the sun in my eyes throughout the day Etc but bare feet on the grass it's one of the things I love about being here is it's easy to be outside I got grass and jungle and all kinds of good stuff here all right guys those are some health hacks in my house hope
